description: Got a big access log at /home/user/logs/access.log — tab-separated, columns are timestamp, ip, method, path, status, bytes. Need to pull just the ip and status columns (2nd and 5th) into /home/user/logs/ip_status.tsv, same order, keep the tabs.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/logs/access.log exists, tab-separated, 6 columns per line: timestamp, ip, method, path, status, bytes.
- File contains 847 lines, no header row.
- Example line: "2024-01-15T09:23:41Z\t192.168.1.42\tGET\t/api/users\t200\t1523"
- /home/user/logs/ is writable.
- /home/user/logs/ip_status.tsv does not exist initially.
- cut, awk, sed, perl available.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/logs/ip_status.tsv exists.
- File contains exactly 847 lines.
- Each line has exactly 2 tab-separated fields: the ip (originally column 2) and the status (originally column 5) from the corresponding line in access.log.
- Fields are separated by a single tab character.
- Line order preserved from original file.

Invariants:
- /home/user/logs/access.log unchanged.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- Line count must match original: `wc -l < /home/user/logs/ip_status.tsv` returns 847.
- Spot check line 1: field 1 of output matches field 2 of input, field 2 of output matches field 5 of input.
- No extra whitespace or field separators: `awk -F'\t' 'NF != 2 { exit 1 }' /home/user/logs/ip_status.tsv` exits 0.
name: task_000000_574e9d68
